Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotAnimation

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotAnimation
public class PotAnimation extends Object
Represents a configurable animation for a presentation object.

This class is immutable and provides a fluent builder API for constructing animation instances. It defines standard animation types (entrance, emphasis, exit) and effects (fade, fly, spin, etc.), along with properties for timing, direction, and repetition. Predefined constants for common animations are provided for convenience.

Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    PotAnimation.Effect
    The specific visual effect of an animation.
    static enum 
    PotAnimation.Trigger
    The event that starts the animation.
    static enum 
    PotAnimation.Type
    The primary category of an animation.

    A predefined entrance animation that makes an object appear instantly.

    A predefined exit animation that makes an object disappear instantly.

  • Method Details

    • entrance

      public static PotAnimation entrance(PotAnimation.Effect effect)
      Creates a new entrance animation with the specified effect.

      The animation will have default properties: triggered on click, 500ms duration, no delay, no repeat, and no auto-reverse.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a fade-in entrance animation
 PotAnimation myFadeIn = PotAnimation.entrance(Effect.FADE);
      Parameters:
      effect - the visual effect for the entrance animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured as an entrance
      See Also:
      • emphasis(Effect)
      • exit(Effect)

    • emphasis

      public static PotAnimation emphasis(PotAnimation.Effect effect)
      Creates a new emphasis animation with the specified effect.

      The animation will have default properties: triggered on click, 500ms duration, no delay, no repeat, and no auto-reverse.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a pulse emphasis animation
 PotAnimation myPulse = PotAnimation.emphasis(Effect.PULSE);
      Parameters:
      effect - the visual effect for the emphasis animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured as an emphasis
      See Also:
      • entrance(Effect)
      • exit(Effect)

    • exit

      public static PotAnimation exit(PotAnimation.Effect effect)
      Creates a new exit animation with the specified effect.

      The animation will have default properties: triggered on click, 500ms duration, no delay, no repeat, and no auto-reverse.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a fade-out exit animation
 PotAnimation myFadeOut = PotAnimation.exit(Effect.FADE);
      Parameters:
      effect - the visual effect for the exit animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured as an exit
      See Also:
      • entrance(Effect)
      • emphasis(Effect)

    • appear

      public static PotAnimation appear()
      Returns a predefined animation that makes an object appear instantly.
      Returns:
      the APPEAR animation constant
      See Also:
      • APPEAR

    • fadeIn

      public static PotAnimation fadeIn()
      Returns a predefined animation that fades an object in.
      Returns:
      the FADE_IN animation constant
      See Also:
      • FADE_IN

    • fadeOut

      public static PotAnimation fadeOut()
      Returns a predefined animation that fades an object out.
      Returns:
      the FADE_OUT animation constant
      See Also:
      • FADE_OUT

    • flyIn

      public static PotAnimation flyIn(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a fly-in entrance animation from the specified direction.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a fly-in animation from the top
 PotAnimation flyDown = PotAnimation.flyIn(PotDirection.FROM_TOP);
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction from which the object flies in
      Returns:
      a new fly-in entrance PotAnimation
      See Also:
      • flyIn()

    • flyIn

      public static PotAnimation flyIn()
      Creates a fly-in entrance animation from the left (default direction).

      This is a convenience method equivalent to flyIn(PotDirection.FROM_LEFT).

      Returns:
      a new fly-in entrance PotAnimation from the left
      See Also:
      • flyIn(PotDirection)

    • flyOut

      public static PotAnimation flyOut(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a fly-out exit animation to the specified direction.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a fly-out animation to the right
 PotAnimation flyRight = PotAnimation.flyOut(PotDirection.FROM_RIGHT);
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction to which the object flies out
      Returns:
      a new fly-out exit PotAnimation

    • zoomIn

      public static PotAnimation zoomIn()
      Returns a predefined animation that zooms an object in.
      Returns:
      the ZOOM_IN animation constant
      See Also:
      • ZOOM_IN

    • zoomOut

      public static PotAnimation zoomOut()
      Returns a predefined animation that zooms an object out.
      Returns:
      the ZOOM_OUT animation constant
      See Also:
      • ZOOM_OUT

    • wipeIn

      public static PotAnimation wipeIn(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a wipe-in entrance animation from the specified direction.
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction from which the wipe effect originates
      Returns:
      a new wipe-in entrance PotAnimation

    • bounceIn

      public static PotAnimation bounceIn()
      Returns a predefined animation that bounces an object in.
      Returns:
      the BOUNCE_IN animation constant
      See Also:
      • BOUNCE_IN

    • spinIn

      public static PotAnimation spinIn()
      Returns a predefined animation that spins an object in.
      Returns:
      the SPIN_IN animation constant
      See Also:
      • SPIN_IN

    • pulse

      public static PotAnimation pulse()
      Returns a predefined emphasis animation that pulses an object.
      Returns:
      the PULSE animation constant
      See Also:
      • PULSE

    • spin

      public static PotAnimation spin()
      Returns a predefined emphasis animation that spins an object.
      Returns:
      the SPIN animation constant
      See Also:
      • SPIN

    • grow

      public static PotAnimation grow()
      Returns a predefined emphasis animation that grows an object.
      Returns:
      the GROW animation constant
      See Also:
      • GROW

    • teeter

      public static PotAnimation teeter()
      Returns a predefined emphasis animation that teeters (rocks) an object.
      Returns:
      the TEETER animation constant
      See Also:
      • TEETER

    • blink

      public static PotAnimation blink()
      Returns a predefined emphasis animation that blinks (flashes once) an object.
      Returns:
      the BLINK animation constant
      See Also:
      • BLINK

    • scale

      public static PotAnimation scale()
      Creates an entrance animation with a zoom effect.

      This method is an alias for entrance(Effect) with PotAnimation.Effect.ZOOM. It is provided as a convenience for API consistency.

      Returns:
      a new zoom-in entrance PotAnimation
      See Also:
      • entrance(Effect)
      • ZOOM_IN

    • duration

      public PotAnimation duration(int milliseconds)
      Returns a copy of this animation with the specified duration in milliseconds.

      If the provided value is negative, it will be treated as 0.

      Usage Example

      
 // Create a 2-second long fade-in
 PotAnimation longFadeIn = PotAnimation.fadeIn().duration(2000);
      Parameters:
      milliseconds - the new duration in milliseconds
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation instance with the updated duration
      See Also:
      • duration(double)

    • duration

      public PotAnimation duration(double seconds)
      Creates a new animation with the specified duration in seconds.

      This is a convenience method that converts the duration from seconds to milliseconds. Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its duration. The duration must be non-negative; a negative value will be treated as zero.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.spin().duration(2.5); // 2.5 second spin
      Parameters:
      seconds - the new duration for the animation in seconds
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified duration

    • delay

      public PotAnimation delay(int milliseconds)
      Creates a new animation with the specified delay before starting.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its start delay. The delay must be non-negative; a negative value will be treated as zero.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.flyIn().delay(500); // Wait 500ms before flying in
      Parameters:
      milliseconds - the delay before the animation starts, in milliseconds
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified delay

    • onClick

      public PotAnimation onClick()
      Creates a new animation configured to be triggered by a click.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except its trigger is set to PotAnimation.Trigger.ON_CLICK.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.pulse().onClick(); // Pulse when clicked
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured to trigger on click
      See Also:
      • PotAnimation.Trigger.ON_CLICK

    • withPrevious

      public PotAnimation withPrevious()
      Creates a new animation configured to start simultaneously with the previous animation.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except its trigger is set to PotAnimation.Trigger.WITH_PREVIOUS.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.fadeIn().withPrevious(); // Start fading in with the prior animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured to trigger with the previous animation
      See Also:
      • PotAnimation.Trigger.WITH_PREVIOUS

    • afterPrevious

      public PotAnimation afterPrevious()
      Creates a new animation configured to start after the previous animation finishes.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except its trigger is set to PotAnimation.Trigger.AFTER_PREVIOUS.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.zoomOut().afterPrevious(); // Zoom out after the prior animation ends
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured to trigger after the previous animation
      See Also:
      • PotAnimation.Trigger.AFTER_PREVIOUS

    • from

      public PotAnimation from(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a new animation with the specified origin direction.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its direction property. This is typically used with entrance effects like PotAnimation.Effect.FLY or PotAnimation.Effect.WIPE to specify where the motion originates.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.flyIn().from(PotDirection.FROM_TOP); // Fly in from the top
      Parameters:
      direction - the origin direction for the animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified origin direction
      See Also:
      • PotDirection

    • direction

      public PotAnimation direction(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a new animation with the specified direction.

      This is an alias for from(PotDirection). Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its direction property.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.wipeIn().direction(PotDirection.FROM_LEFT);
      Parameters:
      direction - the direction for the animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified direction
      See Also:
      • from(PotDirection)

    • to

      public PotAnimation to(PotDirection direction)
      Creates a new animation with the specified target direction.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its direction property. This is typically used with exit effects like PotAnimation.Effect.FLY to specify where the motion ends.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.flyOut().to(PotDirection.FROM_RIGHT); // Fly out to the right
      Parameters:
      direction - the target direction for the animation
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified target direction
      See Also:
      • PotDirection

    • repeat

      public PotAnimation repeat(int count)
      Creates a new animation with the specified repeat count.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its repeat count. A count of zero means the animation plays once (no repeat). A count of -1 indicates infinite repetition. Positive values specify the exact number of additional repetitions.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.pulse().repeat(3); // Pulse four times total
      Parameters:
      count - the number of times to repeat the animation (0 for once, -1 for infinite)
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified repeat count

    • repeatForever

      public PotAnimation repeatForever()
      Creates a new animation configured to repeat indefinitely.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except its repeat count is set to -1.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.spin().repeatForever(); // Spin continuously
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation configured to repeat forever
      See Also:
      • repeat(int)

    • autoReverse

      public PotAnimation autoReverse(boolean autoReverse)
      Creates a new animation with the specified auto-reverse behavior.

      Returns a new PotAnimation instance that is identical to this one except for its auto-reverse flag. When auto-reverse is enabled, the animation will play forward and then in reverse for each cycle.

      Usage Example

      
 PotAnimation animation = PotAnimation.grow().autoReverse(true); // Grow then shrink on each repeat
      Parameters:
      autoReverse - true to enable auto-reverse playback, false otherwise
      Returns:
      a new PotAnimation with the specified auto-reverse behavior

    • getType

      public PotAnimation.Type getType()
      Returns the type of this animation.
      Returns:
      the animation PotAnimation.Type

    • getEffect

      public PotAnimation.Effect getEffect()
      Returns the visual effect of this animation.
      Returns:
      the animation PotAnimation.Effect

    • getTrigger

      public PotAnimation.Trigger getTrigger()
      Returns the trigger condition for this animation.
      Returns:
      the animation PotAnimation.Trigger

    • getDuration

      public int getDuration()
      Returns the duration of this animation in milliseconds.
      Returns:
      the animation duration in milliseconds

    • getDelay

      public int getDelay()
      Returns the delay before this animation starts, in milliseconds.
      Returns:
      the start delay in milliseconds

    • getDirection

      public PotDirection getDirection()
      Returns the direction associated with this animation.
      Returns:
      the animation direction, or null if not applicable

    • getRepeatCount

      public int getRepeatCount()
      Returns the repeat count for this animation.
      Returns:
      the repeat count (0 for once, -1 for infinite, positive for specific count)

    • isAutoReverse

      public boolean isAutoReverse()
      Returns whether this animation uses auto-reverse playback.
      Returns:
      true if auto-reverse is enabled, false otherwise

    • isEntrance

      public boolean isEntrance()
      Checks if this animation is an entrance type.
      Returns:
      true if the animation type is PotAnimation.Type.ENTRANCE, false otherwise

    • isEmphasis

      public boolean isEmphasis()
      Checks if this animation is an emphasis type.
      Returns:
      true if the animation type is PotAnimation.Type.EMPHASIS, false otherwise

    • isExit

      public boolean isExit()
      Checks if this animation is an exit type.
      Returns:
      true if the animation type is PotAnimation.Type.EXIT, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Compares this animation to the specified object for equality.

      Two PotAnimation instances are considered equal if all their configurable properties (type, effect, trigger, duration, delay, direction, repeat count, and auto-reverse) are equal.

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this animation.
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      a hash code based on the animation's properties

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this animation.

      The string includes the animation's type, effect, trigger, duration, and any non-default properties such as delay, direction, and repeat count.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a descriptive string representation of this animation
